Class PosInSequentTransferable

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.nodeviews.PosInSequentTransferable
All Implemented Interfaces:
Transferable
public class PosInSequentTransferable extends Object implements Transferable
This class in an implementation of the Transferable interface and allows to transfer a PosInSequent object. It supports to data flavors:
  • POS_IN_SEQUENT_TRANSFER flavor which is of mime type DataFlavor.javaJVMLocalObjectMimeType
  • DataFlavor.stringFlavor which returns the term described by the PosInSequent as a parsable string

  • Field Details

    • POS_IN_SEQUENT_TRANSFER

      public static DataFlavor POS_IN_SEQUENT_TRANSFER

  • Constructor Details

    • PosInSequentTransferable

      public PosInSequentTransferable(PosInSequent pis, Services serv)
      creates an instance of this transferable
      Parameters:
      pis - the PosInSequent to be transfered (string flavor only supported if pis denotes a term or formula, not the complete sequent)

  • Method Details

    • getTransferDataFlavors

      public DataFlavor[] getTransferDataFlavors()
      returns the supported flavors of this transferable. These are currently DataFlavor.stringFlavor and POS_IN_SEQUENT_TRANSFER
      Specified by:
      getTransferDataFlavors in interface Transferable
      See Also:
      • Transferable.getTransferDataFlavors()

    • isDataFlavorSupported

      public boolean isDataFlavorSupported(DataFlavor flavor)
      Specified by:
      isDataFlavorSupported in interface Transferable

    • getTransferData

      public Object getTransferData(DataFlavor flavor) throws UnsupportedFlavorException, IOException
      if the flavor is equal to the POS_IN_SEQUENT_TRANSFER the return data is of kind PosInSequent. If the flavor equals DataFlavor.stringFlavor the highlighted term is returned as parsable string.
      Specified by:
      getTransferData in interface Transferable
      Throws:
      UnsupportedFlavorException - if the flavor is not supported
      IOException
